title: Spengler bilingual glossary — Man and Technics translation
purpose: The term-consistency contract for our fresh English translation of Der Mensch und die Technik. Headline terms (lexicon:true) are fixed to the renderings already used across the portal's 40-term lexicon and 242 annotations, so the new translation speaks the portal's established Spengler vocabulary. Working terms are recurring M&T words rendered consistently. Derived from data/lexicon/spengler.lexicon.json (german fields) + the German<->English Decline corpus.
conventions: Sperrung (letter-spaced emphasis in the German, e.g. 'K u l t u r') -> render as italics.
Spengler's capitalized abstractions (Kultur, Zivilisation, Geschichte als Subjekt) keep their weight; Culture/Civilization are capitalized as in the lexicon.
Keep his long periodic sentences where English can carry them; break only when the period would snap in English.
{N} markers are the original 1931 Beck page numbers — provenance only, dropped from reading text.
Translate from the German; consult Atkinson only as one reference, never as a base to paraphrase.

terms.note: NOT 'technology'; Spengler's wide sense — the tactics of living.
the book's master-definition of technics.
capitalized; the master-opposition.
man as Raubtier; render as praise where Spengler means it so.
the plant-eater, ranked below the predator.
Werkzeug = tool/implement.
'collective doing by plan'; unternehmen = to undertake.
Maschinenkultur -> machine-culture; Maschinentechnik -> machine-technics.
Nietzsche's, adopted by Spengler.
the time-logic of history; 'fate' only where the tone is doom.
the Culture-soul; 'Von der Seele war nicht die Rede' = 'of the soul there was no word.'
Being (cosmic), against Wachsein.
waking-consciousness, the sensing surface.
Spengler's own phrase ('wie ich das bezeichnet habe').
homology across Cultures.
the Decline = Der Untergang des Abendlandes.
Geschichte als Subjekt keeps its weight.
die Hybris des Prometheus -> the hubris of Prometheus.
Entstehen und Vergehen -> coming-to-be and passing-away.
later 'the priest of the machine'.
the doing, against the thing made.
'nicht die Waffe, sondern der Kampf' = not the weapon but the battle.
life as Kampf; ohne Gnade = without mercy.
the first true deed, against mere animal activity.
das Wesen des Technischen -> the essence of the technical.
Spengler's non-biological 'race'; handle with the lexicon's critical framing.
Fortschrittsphilister -> progress-philistine.
Weltstadt is the lexicon term.
of technics, against the inventor.
keep the Latin; Spengler's own.
keep the Latin.
